verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc congregati sunt filii Moab et filii Ammon, et cum eis de Ammonitis, ad Josaphat, ut pugnarent contra eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י אַחֲרֵי־כֵ֡ן בָּ֣אוּ בְנֵי־מוֹאָב֩ וּבְנֵ֨י עַמּ֜וֹן וְעִמָּהֶ֧ם ׀ מֵהָעַמּוֹנִ֛ים עַל־יְהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט לַמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: After this the children of Moab, and the children of Ammon, and with them of the Ammonites, were gathered together to fight against Josaphat.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte tulivat Moabilaiset, Ammonilaiset ja heidän kanssansa myös muita, paitsi Ammonilaisia, sotimaan Josaphatia vastaan.
verses.king_james: It came to pass after this also, that the children of Moab, and the children of Ammon, and with them other beside the Ammonites, came against Jehoshaphat to battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veneruntque nuntii, et indicaverunt Josaphat, dicentes : Venit contra te multitudo magna de his locis quæ trans mare sunt, et de Syria : et ecce consistunt in Asasonthamar, quæ est Engaddi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֗אוּ וַיַּגִּ֤ידוּ לִיהֽוֹשָׁפָט֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר בָּ֣א עָלֶ֜יךָ הָמ֥וֹן רָ֛ב מֵעֵ֥בֶר לַיָּ֖ם מֵאֲרָ֑ם וְהִנָּם֙ בְּחַֽצְצ֣וֹן תָּמָ֔ר הִ֖יא עֵ֥ין גֶּֽדִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there came messengers, and told Josaphat, saying: There cometh a great multitude against thee from beyond the sea, and out of Syria, and behold they are in Asasonthamar, which is Engaddi.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tultiin ja ilmoitettiin Josaphatille, sanoen: sinua vastaan tulee sangen suuri sotajoukko Syriasta, tuolta puolelta meren; ja katso, he ovat HatsetsonTamarissa: se on Engeddi.
verses.king_james: Then there came some that told Jehoshaphat, saying, There cometh a great multitude against thee from beyond the sea on this side Syria; and, behold, they be in Hazazon–tamar, which is En–gedi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Josaphat autem timore perterritus, totum se contulit ad rogandum Dominum, et prædicavit jejunium universo Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרָ֕א וַיִּתֵּ֧ן יְהוֹשָׁפָ֛ט אֶת־פָּנָ֖יו לִדְר֣וֹשׁ לַֽיהֹוָ֑ה וַיִּקְרָא־צ֖וֹם עַל־כׇּל־יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Josaphat being seized with fear betook himself wholly to pray to the Lord, and he proclaimed a fast for all Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Josaphat pelkäsi, ja asetti kasvonsa etsimään Herraa, ja antoi kuuluttaa paaston koko Juudassa.
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat feared, and set himself to seek the Lord, and proclaimed a fast throughout all Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congregatusque est Judas ad deprecandum Dominum : sed et omnes de urbibus suis venerunt ad obsecrandum eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקָּבְצ֣וּ יְהוּדָ֔ה לְבַקֵּ֖שׁ מֵיְהֹוָ֑ה גַּ֚ם מִכׇּל־עָרֵ֣י יְהוּדָ֔ה בָּ֖אוּ לְבַקֵּ֥שׁ אֶת־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Juda gathered themselves together to pray to the Lord: and all came out of their cities to make supplication to him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Juuda tuli kokoon etsimään Herralta; tultiin myös kaikista Juudan kaupungeista etsimään Herraa.
verses.king_james: And Judah gathered themselves together, to ask help of the Lord: even out of all the cities of Judah they came to seek the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque stetisset Josaphat in medio cœtu Juda et Jerusalem, in domo Domini ante atrium novum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲמֹ֣ד יְהוֹשָׁפָ֗ט בִּקְהַ֧ל יְהוּדָ֛ה וִירוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם בְּבֵ֣ית יְהֹוָ֑ה לִפְנֵ֖י הֶחָצֵ֥ר הַחֲדָשָֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Josaphat stood in the midst of the assembly of Juda, and Jerusalem, in the house of the Lord before the new court,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josaphat seisoi Juudan ja Jerusalemin seurakunnassa, Herran huoneessa, uuden pihan edessä.
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat stood in the congregation of Judah and Jerusalem, in the house of the Lord, before the new court,

verses.latin_vulgate: ait : Domine Deus patrum nostrorum, tu es Deus in cælo, et dominaris cunctis regnis gentium : in manu tua est fortitudo et potentia, nec quisquam tibi potest resistere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמַ֗ר יְהֹוָ֞ה אֱלֹהֵ֤י אֲבֹתֵ֙ינוּ֙ הֲלֹ֨א אַתָּה־ה֤וּא אֱלֹהִים֙ בַּשָּׁמַ֔יִם וְאַתָּ֣ה מוֹשֵׁ֔ל בְּכֹ֖ל מַמְלְכ֣וֹת הַגּוֹיִ֑ם וּבְיָֽדְךָ֙ כֹּ֣חַ וּגְבוּרָ֔ה וְאֵ֥ין עִמְּךָ֖ לְהִתְיַצֵּֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And said: O Lord God of our fathers, thou art God in heaven, and rulest over all the kingdoms and nations, in thy hand is strength and power, and no one can resist thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: Herra meidän isäimme Jumala! etkös ole Jumala taivaissa, joka hallitset kaikkein pakanain valtakunnissa? Ja sinun kädessäs on väki ja voima, ja ei ole yhtään, joka voi olla sinua vastaan.
verses.king_james: And said, O Lord God of our fathers, art not thou God in heaven? and rulest not thou over all the kingdoms of the heathen? and in thine hand is there not power and might, so that none is able to withstand thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne tu, Deus noster, interfecisti omnes habitatores terræ hujus coram populo tuo Israël, et dedisti eam semini Abraham amici tui in sempiternum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹ֣א ׀ אַתָּ֣ה אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ הוֹרַ֙שְׁתָּ֙ אֶת־יֹֽשְׁבֵי֙ הָאָ֣רֶץ הַזֹּ֔את מִלִּפְנֵ֖י עַמְּךָ֣ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַֽתִּתְּנָ֗הּ לְזֶ֛רַע אַבְרָהָ֥ם אֹהַבְךָ֖ לְעוֹלָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Didst not thou our God kill all the inhabitants of this land before thy people Israel, and gavest it to the seed of Abraham thy friend for ever?
verses.finnish_biblia: Etkös sinä, meidän Jumalamme, ole ajanut tämän maan asujamia kansas Israelin edestä pois? ja olet sen antanut Abrahamin sinun ystäväs siemenelle ijankaikkisesti;
verses.king_james: Art not thou our God, who didst drive out the inhabitants of this land before thy people Israel, and gavest it to the seed of Abraham thy friend for ever?

verses.latin_vulgate: Habitaveruntque in ea, et exstruxerunt in illa sanctuarium nomini tuo, dicentes :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּשְׁב֖וּ־בָ֑הּ וַיִּבְנ֨וּ לְךָ֧ ׀ בָּ֛הּ מִקְדָּ֖שׁ לְשִׁמְךָ֥ לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they dwelt in it, and built in it a sanctuary to thy name, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin että he ovat asuneet siinä, ja ovat rakentaneet sinun nimelles pyhän, sanoen:
verses.king_james: And they dwelt therein, and have built thee a sanctuary therein for thy name, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Si irruerint super nos mala, gladius judicii, pestilentia, et fames, stabimus coram domo hac in conspectu tuo, in qua invocatum est nomen tuum : et clamabimus ad te in tribulationibus nostris, et exaudies, salvosque facies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־תָּב֨וֹא עָלֵ֜ינוּ רָעָ֗ה חֶ֘רֶב֮ שְׁפוֹט֮ וְדֶ֣בֶר וְרָעָב֒ נַעַמְדָ֞ה לִפְנֵ֨י הַבַּ֤יִת הַזֶּה֙ וּלְפָנֶ֔יךָ כִּ֥י שִׁמְךָ֖ בַּבַּ֣יִת הַזֶּ֑ה וְנִזְעַ֥ק אֵלֶ֛יךָ מִצָּרָתֵ֖נוּ וְתִשְׁמַ֥ע וְתוֹשִֽׁיעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: If evils fall upon us, the sword of judgment, or pestilence, or famine, we will stand in thy presence before this house, in which thy name is called upon: and we will cry to thee in our afflictions, and thou wilt hear, and save us.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin joku onnettomuus, rangaistusmiekka, rutto eli kallis aika meille tulee, niin pitää meidän seisoman tässä huoneessa sinun edessäs (sillä sinun nimes on tässä huoneessa) ja huutaman sinua tuskassamme, niin sinä kuulet ja autat.
verses.king_james: If, when evil cometh upon us, as the sword, judgment, or pestilence, or famine, we stand before this house, and in thy presence, (for thy name is in this house,) and cry unto thee in our affliction, then thou wilt hear and help.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc igitur, ecce filii Ammon, et Moab, et mons Seir, per quos non concessisti Israël ut transirent quando egrediebantur de Ægypto, sed declinaverunt ab eis, et non interfecerunt illos,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֡ה הִנֵּה֩ בְנֵי־עַמּ֨וֹן וּמוֹאָ֜ב וְהַר־שֵׂעִ֗יר אֲ֠שֶׁ֠ר לֹֽא־נָתַ֤תָּה לְיִשְׂרָאֵל֙ לָב֣וֹא בָהֶ֔ם בְּבֹאָ֖ם מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרָ֑יִם כִּ֛י סָ֥רוּ מֵעֲלֵיהֶ֖ם וְלֹ֥א הִשְׁמִידֽוּם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now therefore behold the children of Ammon, and of Moab, and mount Seir, through whose lands thou didst not allow Israel to pass, when they came out of Egypt, but they turned aside from them, and slew them not,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja nyt katso, Ammonin lapset, Moab ja Seirin vuorelaiset, joidenka kautta et sinä antanut Israelin lasten mennä, vaeltaissansa Egyptistä; vaan heidän täytyi palata pois heidän tyköänsä, eikä hävittäneet heitä;
verses.king_james: And now, behold, the children of Ammon and Moab and mount Seir, whom thou wouldest not let Israel invade, when they came out of the land of Egypt, but they turned from them, and destroyed them not;

verses.latin_vulgate: e contrario agunt, et nituntur ejicere nos de possessione quam tradidisti nobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִ֨נֵּה־הֵ֔ם גֹּמְלִ֖ים עָלֵ֑ינוּ לָבוֹא֙ לְגָ֣רְשֵׁ֔נוּ מִיְּרֻשָּׁתְךָ֖ אֲשֶׁ֥ר הוֹרַשְׁתָּֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Do the contrary, and endeavour to cast us out of the possession which thou hast delivered to us.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katso, he kostavat sen meille; ja he tulevat meitä ajamaan perinnöstäs pois, jonka meille perinnöksi antanut olet.
verses.king_james: Behold, I say, how they reward us, to come to cast us out of thy possession, which thou hast given us to inherit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus noster, ergo non judicabis eos ? in nobis quidem non est tanta fortitudo, ut possimus huic multitudini resistere, quæ irruit super nos. Sed cum ignoremus quid agere debeamus, hoc solum habemus residui, ut oculos nostros dirigamus ad te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱלֹהֵ֙ינוּ֙ הֲלֹ֣א תִשְׁפׇּט־בָּ֔ם כִּ֣י אֵ֥ין בָּ֙נוּ֙ כֹּ֔חַ לִ֠פְנֵ֠י הֶהָמ֥וֹן הָרָ֛ב הַזֶּ֖ה הַבָּ֣א עָלֵ֑ינוּ וַאֲנַ֗חְנוּ לֹ֤א נֵדַע֙ מַֽה־נַּעֲשֶׂ֔ה כִּ֥י עָלֶ֖יךָ עֵינֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: O our God, wilt thou not then judge them? as for us we have not strength enough, to be able to resist this multitude, which cometh violently upon us. But as we know not what to do, we can only turn our eyes to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Meidän Jumalamme! etkös heitä tuomitse? Sillä ei ole meillä yhtään voimaa tätä suurta joukkoa vastaan, joka tulee meitä vastaan: emme tiedä, mitä me teemme, vaan meidän silmämme katselevat sinun puolees.
verses.king_james: O our God, wilt thou not judge them? for we have no might against this great company that cometh against us; neither know we what to do: but our eyes are upon thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis vero Juda stabat coram Domino cum parvulis, et uxoribus, et liberis suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכׇ֨ל־יְהוּדָ֔ה עֹמְדִ֖ים לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה גַּם־טַפָּ֖ם נְשֵׁיהֶ֥ם וּבְנֵיהֶֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And all Juda stood before the Lord with their little ones, and their wives, and their children.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki Juuda seisoi Herran edessä, lapsinensa, vaimoinensa ja poikinensa.
verses.king_james: And all Judah stood before the Lord, with their little ones, their wives, and their children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem Jahaziel filius Zachariæ filii Banaiæ filii Jehiel filii Mathaniæ, Levites de filiis Asaph, super quem factus est spiritus Domini, in medio turbæ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיַחֲזִיאֵ֡ל בֶּן־זְכַרְיָ֡הוּ בֶּן־בְּ֠נָיָ֠ה בֶּן־יְעִיאֵ֧ל בֶּן־מַתַּנְיָ֛ה הַלֵּוִ֖י מִן־בְּנֵ֣י אָסָ֑ף הָיְתָ֤ה עָלָיו֙ ר֣וּחַ יְהֹוָ֔ה בְּת֖וֹךְ הַקָּהָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jahaziel the son of Zacharias, the son of Banaias, the son of Jehiel, the son of Mathanias, a Levite of the sons of Asaph, was there, upon whom the spirit of the Lord came in the midst of the multitude,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jehasielin Sakarian pojan, Benajan pojan, Jehielin pojan, Mattanjan pojan päälle, Leviläisen Asaphin lapsista, tuli Herran henki seurakunnan keskellä,
verses.king_james: Then upon Jahaziel the son of Zechariah, the son of Benaiah, the son of Jeiel, the son of Mattaniah, a Levite of the sons of Asaph, came the Spirit of the Lord in the midst of the congregation;

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait : Attendite, omnis Juda, et qui habitatis Jerusalem, et tu, rex Josaphat : hæc dicit Dominus vobis : Nolite timere, nec paveatis hanc multitudinem : non est enim vestra pugna, sed Dei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הַקְשִׁ֤יבוּ כׇל־יְהוּדָה֙ וְיֹשְׁבֵ֣י יְרוּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם וְהַמֶּ֖לֶךְ יְהוֹשָׁפָ֑ט כֹּה־אָמַ֨ר יְהֹוָ֜ה לָכֶ֗ם אַ֠תֶּ֠ם אַל־תִּֽירְא֤וּ וְאַל־תֵּחַ֙תּוּ֙ מִפְּנֵ֨י הֶהָמ֤וֹן הָרָב֙ הַזֶּ֔ה כִּ֣י לֹ֥א לָכֶ֛ם הַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה כִּ֥י לֵאלֹהִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: Attend ye, all Juda, and you that dwell in Jerusalem, and thou king Josaphat: Thus saith the Lord to you: Fear ye not, and be not dismayed at this multitude: for the battle is not yours, but God's.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi: kuulkaat, koko Juuda ja Jerusalemin asuvaiset, ja kuningas Josaphat! Näin sanoo Herra teille: älkäät peljätkö, älkäät vavisko tätä suurta joukkoa, sillä te ette sodi, vaan Jumala.
verses.king_james: And he said, Hearken ye, all Judah, and ye inhabitants of Jerusalem, and thou king Jehoshaphat, Thus saith the Lord unto you, Be not afraid nor dismayed by reason of this great multitude; for the battle is not yours, but God’s.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cras descendetis contra eos : ascensuri enim sunt per clivum nomine Sis, et invenietis illos in summitate torrentis qui est contra solitudinem Jeruel.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מָחָר֙ רְד֣וּ עֲלֵיהֶ֔ם הִנָּ֥ם עֹלִ֖ים בְּמַעֲלֵ֣ה הַצִּ֑יץ וּמְצָאתֶ֤ם אֹתָם֙ בְּס֣וֹף הַנַּ֔חַל פְּנֵ֖י מִדְבַּ֥ר יְרוּאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: To morrow you shall go down against them: for they will come up by the ascent named Sis, and you shall find them at the head of the torrent, which is over against the wilderness of Jeruel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Huomenna pitää teidän menemän alas heidän tykönsä, ja katso, he menevät ylös Zitsin paltaan, ja te löydätte heidät ojan lopulla kohdastansa Jeruelin korven edessä.
verses.king_james: To morrow go ye down against them: behold, they come up by the cliff of Ziz; and ye shall find them at the end of the brook, before the wilderness of Jeruel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non eritis vos qui dimicabitis, sed tantummodo confidenter state, et videbitis auxilium Domini super vos, o Juda et Jerusalem : nolite timere, nec paveatis : cras egrediemini contra eos, et Dominus erit vobiscum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֥א לָכֶ֖ם לְהִלָּחֵ֣ם בָּזֹ֑את הִתְיַצְּב֣וּ עִמְד֡וּ וּרְא֣וּ אֶת־יְשׁוּעַת֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה עִמָּכֶ֜ם יְהוּדָ֣ה וִירוּשָׁלַ֗͏ִם אַל־תִּֽירְאוּ֙ וְאַל־תֵּחַ֔תּוּ מָחָר֙ צְא֣וּ לִפְנֵיהֶ֔ם וַיהֹוָ֖ה עִמָּכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: It shall not be you that shall fight, but only stand with confidence, and you shall see the help of the Lord over you, O Juda, and Jerusalem: fear ye not, nor be you dismayed: to morrow you shall go out against them, and the Lord will be with you.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei teidän pidä sotiman tässä asiassa, ainoastansa käykäät edes, seisokaat ja katsokaat Herran autuutta, joka on teidän kanssanne: Juuda ja Jerusalem, älkäät peljätkö ja älkäät hämmästykö, menkäät huomenna ulos heitä vastaan, Herra on teidän kanssanne.
verses.king_james: Ye shall not need to fight in this battle: set yourselves, stand ye still, and see the salvation of the Lord with you, O Judah and Jerusalem: fear not, nor be dismayed; to morrow go out against them: for the Lord will be with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Josaphat ergo, et Juda, et omnes habitatores Jerusalem ceciderunt proni in terram coram Domino, et adoraverunt eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקֹּ֧ד יְהוֹשָׁפָ֛ט אַפַּ֖יִם אָ֑רְצָה וְכׇל־יְהוּדָ֞ה וְיֹשְׁבֵ֣י יְרוּשָׁלַ֗͏ִם נָֽפְלוּ֙ לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה לְהִֽשְׁתַּחֲוֺ֖ת לַיהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Josaphat, and Juda, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem fell flat on the ground before the Lord, and adored him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin kumarsi Josaphat kasvoillensa maahan; ja kaikki Juuda ja Jerusalemin asuvaiset lankesivat Herran eteen ja rukoilivat Herraa.
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat bowed his head with his face to the ground: and all Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem fell before the Lord, worshipping the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Levitæ de filiis Caath et de filiis Core laudabant Dominum Deum Israël voce magna in excelsum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּקֻ֧מוּ הַלְוִיִּ֛ם מִן־בְּנֵ֥י הַקְּהָתִ֖ים וּמִן־בְּנֵ֣י הַקׇּרְחִ֑ים לְהַלֵּ֗ל לַֽיהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל בְּק֥וֹל גָּד֖וֹל לְמָֽעְלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Levites of the sons of Caath, and of the sons of Core praised the Lord the God of Israel with a loud voice, on high.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Leviläiset Kahatilaisten lapsista ja Korhilaisten lapsista nousivat kiittämään Herraa Israelin Jumalaa kovalla äänellä korkeuteen päin.
verses.king_james: And the Levites, of the children of the Kohathites, and of the children of the Korhites, stood up to praise the Lord God of Israel with a loud voice on high.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque mane surrexissent, egressi sunt per desertum Thecue : profectisque eis, stans Josaphat in medio eorum, dixit : Audite me, viri Juda, et omnes habitatores Jerusalem : credite in Domino Deo vestro, et securi eritis : credite prophetis ejus, et cuncta evenient prospera.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשְׁכִּ֣ימוּ בַבֹּ֔קֶר וַיֵּצְא֖וּ לְמִדְבַּ֣ר תְּק֑וֹעַ וּבְצֵאתָ֞ם עָמַ֣ד יְהוֹשָׁפָ֗ט וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ שְׁמָע֗וּנִי יְהוּדָה֙ וְיֹשְׁבֵ֣י יְרוּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם הַאֲמִ֜ינוּ בַּֽיהֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹֽהֵיכֶם֙ וְתֵ֣אָמֵ֔נוּ הַאֲמִ֥ינוּ בִנְבִיאָ֖יו וְהַצְלִֽיחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they rose early in the morning, and went out through the desert of Thecua: and as they were marching, Josaphat standing in the midst of them, said: Hear me, ye men of Juda, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem: believe in the Lord your God, and you shall be secure: believe his prophets, and all things shall succeed well.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he nousivat varhain huomeneltain ja menivät Tekoan korpeen. Ja heidän lähteissänsä seisoi Josaphat ja sanoi: kuulkaat minua, Juuda ja Jerusalemin asujamet, uskokaat Herran teidän Jumalanne päälle, niin te olette hyvässä turvassa, ja uskokaat hänen prophetansa, niin te menestytte.
verses.king_james: And they rose early in the morning, and went forth into the wilderness of Tekoa: and as they went forth, Jehoshaphat stood and said, Hear me, O Judah, and ye inhabitants of Jerusalem; Believe in the Lord your God, so shall ye be established; believe his prophets, so shall ye prosper.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deditque consilium populo, et statuit cantores Domini ut laudarent eum in turmis suis, et antecederent exercitum, ac voce consona dicerent : Confitemini Domino quoniam in æternum misericordia ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּוָּעַץ֙ אֶל־הָעָ֔ם וַיַּעֲמֵ֤ד מְשֹֽׁרְרִים֙ לַיהֹוָ֔ה וּֽמְהַלְלִ֖ים לְהַדְרַת־קֹ֑דֶשׁ בְּצֵאת֙ לִפְנֵ֣י הֶחָל֔וּץ וְאֹֽמְרִים֙ הוֹד֣וּ לַיהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֥י לְעוֹלָ֖ם חַסְדּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he gave counsel to the people, and appointed the singing men of the Lord, to praise him by their companies, and to go before the army, and with one voice to say: Give glory to the Lord, for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän keskusteli kansan kanssa, ja asetti veisaajat Herralle, kiittämään pyhässä kaunistuksessa, mennessä hankitun sotaväen edellä, jotka sanoivat: kiittäkäät Herraa, sillä hänen laupiutensa pysyy ijankaikkisesti.
verses.king_james: And when he had consulted with the people, he appointed singers unto the Lord, and that should praise the beauty of holiness, as they went out before the army, and to say, Praise the Lord; for his mercy endureth for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque cœpissent laudes canere, vertit Dominus insidias eorum in semetipsos, filiorum scilicet Ammon, et Moab, et montis Seir, qui egressi fuerant ut pugnarent contra Judam : et percussi sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְעֵת֩ הֵחֵ֨לּוּ בְרִנָּ֜ה וּתְהִלָּ֗ה נָתַ֣ן יְהֹוָ֣ה ׀ מְ֠אָֽרְבִ֠ים עַל־בְּנֵ֨י עַמּ֜וֹן מוֹאָ֧ב וְהַר־שֵׂעִ֛יר הַבָּאִ֥ים לִֽיהוּדָ֖ה וַיִּנָּגֵֽפוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they began to sing praises, the Lord turned their ambushments upon themselves, that is to say, of the children of Ammon, and of Moab, and of mount Seir, who were come out to fight against Juda, and they were slain.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sittekuin he rupesivat kiittämään ja ylistämään, antoi Herra väijyjät tulla Ammonin, Moabin ja Seirin vuorelaisten päälle, jotka Juudaa vastaan tulleet olivat; ja he löivät heidät.
verses.king_james: And when they began to sing and to praise, the Lord set ambushments against the children of Ammon, Moab, and mount Seir, which were come against Judah; and they were smitten.

verses.latin_vulgate: Namque filii Ammon et Moab consurrexerunt adversum habitatores montis Seir, ut interficerent et delerent eos : cumque hoc opere perpetrassent, etiam in semetipsos versi, mutuis concidere vulneribus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֠יַּעַמְד֠וּ בְּנֵ֨י עַמּ֧וֹן וּמוֹאָ֛ב עַל־יֹשְׁבֵ֥י הַר־שֵׂעִ֖יר לְהַחֲרִ֣ים וּלְהַשְׁמִ֑יד וּכְכַלּוֹתָם֙ בְּיוֹשְׁבֵ֣י שֵׂעִ֔יר עָזְר֥וּ אִישׁ־בְּרֵעֵ֖הוּ לְמַשְׁחִֽית׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the children of Ammon, and of Moab, rose up against the inhabitants of mount Seir, to kill and destroy them: and when they had made an end of them, they turned also against one another, and destroyed one another.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin nousivat Ammonin ja Moabin lapset Seirin vuoren asuvia vastaan, tappamaan ja hävittämään heitä. Ja sittekuin he olivat lopettaneet Seirin asuvaiset, autti kukin lähimmäistänsä toinen toistansa tappamaan.
verses.king_james: For the children of Ammon and Moab stood up against the inhabitants of mount Seir, utterly to slay and destroy them: and when they had made an end of the inhabitants of Seir, every one helped to destroy another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Juda, cum venisset ad speculam quæ respicit solitudinem, vidit procul omnem late regionem plenam cadaveribus, nec superesse quemquam qui necem potuisset evadere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וִיהוּדָ֛ה בָּ֥א עַל־הַמִּצְפֶּ֖ה לַמִּדְבָּ֑ר וַיִּפְנוּ֙ אֶל־הֶ֣הָמ֔וֹן וְהִנָּ֧ם פְּגָרִ֛ים נֹפְלִ֥ים אַ֖רְצָה וְאֵ֥ין פְּלֵיטָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Juda came to the watch tower, that looketh toward the desert, they saw afar off all the country, for a great space, full of dead bodies, and that no one was left that could escape death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin Juuda tuli Mitspaan, joka on korven tykönä, käänsivät he heitänsä joukkoa päin, ja katso, silloin makasivat kuolleet ruumiit maassa, niin ettei yksikään heistä ollut päässyt.
verses.king_james: And when Judah came toward the watch tower in the wilderness, they looked unto the multitude, and, behold, they were dead bodies fallen to the earth, and none escaped.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit ergo Josaphat, et omnis populus cum eo, ad detrahenda spolia mortuorum : inveneruntque inter cadavera variam supellectilem, vestes quoque, et vasa pretiosissima, et diripuerunt ita ut omnia portare non possent, nec per tres dies spolia auferre præ prædæ magnitudine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֨א יְהוֹשָׁפָ֣ט וְעַמּוֹ֮ לָבֹ֣ז אֶת־שְׁלָלָם֒ וַיִּמְצְאוּ֩ בָהֶ֨ם לָרֹ֜ב וּרְכ֤וּשׁ וּפְגָרִים֙ וּכְלֵ֣י חֲמֻד֔וֹת וַיְנַצְּל֥וּ לָהֶ֖ם לְאֵ֣ין מַשָּׂ֑א וַיִּהְי֞וּ יָמִ֧ים שְׁלוֹשָׁ֛ה בֹּזְזִ֥ים אֶת־הַשָּׁלָ֖ל כִּ֥י רַב־הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Josaphat came, and all the people with him to take away the spoils of the dead, and they found among the dead bodies, stuff of various kinds, and garments, and most precious vessels: and they took them for themselves, insomuch that they could not carry all, nor in three days take away the spoils, the booty was so great.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josaphat tuli väkinensä jakamaan heidän saalistansa, ja he löysivät siellä ruumisten seassa tavaraa ja kallista kalua, ja he ottivat heiltä niin paljon, ettei he voineet kantaa; ja he ottivat siitä saaliista kolme päivää; sillä sitä oli sangen paljo.
verses.king_james: And when Jehoshaphat and his people came to take away the spoil of them, they found among them in abundance both riches with the dead bodies, and precious jewels, which they stripped off for themselves, more than they could carry away: and they were three days in gathering of the spoil, it was so much.

verses.latin_vulgate: Die autem quarto congregati sunt in Valle benedictionis : etenim quoniam ibi benedixerant Domino, vocaverunt locum illum Vallis benedictionis usque in præsentem diem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבַיּ֣וֹם הָרְבִעִ֗י נִקְהֲלוּ֙ לְעֵ֣מֶק בְּרָכָ֔ה כִּי־שָׁ֖ם בֵּרְכ֣וּ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֑ה עַל־כֵּ֡ן קָרְא֞וּ אֶת־שֵׁ֨ם הַמָּק֥וֹם הַה֛וּא עֵ֥מֶק בְּרָכָ֖ה עַד־הַיּֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And on the fourth day they were assembled in the valley of Blessing: for there they blessed the Lord, and therefore they called that place the valley of Blessing until this day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Neljäntenä päivänä tulivat he kokoon Kiitoslaaksoon, sillä siinä he kiittivät Herraa; siitä kutsutaan se paikka Kiitoslaaksoksi tähän päivään asti.
verses.king_james: And on the fourth day they assembled themselves in the valley of Berachah; for there they blessed the Lord: therefore the name of the same place was called, The valley of Berachah, unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversusque est omnis vir Juda, et habitatores Jerusalem, et Josaphat ante eos, in Jerusalem cum lætitia magna, eo quod dedisset eis Dominus gaudium de inimicis suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֠יָּשֻׁב֠וּ כׇּל־אִ֨ישׁ יְהוּדָ֤ה וִירוּשָׁלַ֙͏ִם֙ וִיהוֹשָׁפָ֣ט בְּרֹאשָׁ֔ם לָשׁ֥וּב אֶל־יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם בְּשִׂמְחָ֑ה כִּֽי־שִׂמְּחָ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה מֵאוֹיְבֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And every man of Juda, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem returned, and Josaphat at their head, into Jerusalem with great joy, because the Lord had made them rejoice over their enemies.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki Juuda ja Jerusalem palasivat takaperin, ja Josaphat kaikkein ensin, niin että he menivät ilolla Jerusalemiin; sillä Herra oli antanut heille ilon heidän vihollisistansa.
verses.king_james: Then they returned, every man of Judah and Jerusalem, and Jehoshaphat in the forefront of them, to go again to Jerusalem with joy; for the Lord had made them to rejoice over their enemies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ingressique sunt in Jerusalem cum psalteriis, et citharis, et tubis in domum Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם בִּנְבָלִ֥ים וּבְכִנֹּר֖וֹת וּבַחֲצֹֽצְר֑וֹת אֶל־בֵּ֖ית יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they came into Jerusalem with psalteries, and harps, and trumpets into the house of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he menivät Jerusalemiin psaltareilla, harpuilla ja basunilla, Herran huoneesen.
verses.king_james: And they came to Jerusalem with psalteries and harps and trumpets unto the house of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Irruit autem pavor Domini super universa regna terrarum cum audissent quod pugnasset Dominus contra inimicos Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ פַּ֣חַד אֱלֹהִ֔ים עַ֖ל כׇּל־מַמְלְכ֣וֹת הָאֲרָצ֑וֹת בְּשׇׁמְעָ֕ם כִּ֚י נִלְחַ֣ם יְהֹוָ֔ה עִ֖ם אוֹיְבֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the fear of the Lord fell upon all the kingdoms of the lands when they heard that the Lord had fought against the enemies of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jumalan pelko tuli kaikkein valtakuntain päälle maalla, sittekuin he kuulivat Herran sotineen Israelin vihollisia vastaan.
verses.king_james: And the fear of God was on all the kingdoms of those countries, when they had heard that the Lord fought against the enemies of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quievitque regnum Josaphat, et præbuit ei Deus pacem per circuitum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּשְׁקֹ֖ט מַלְכ֣וּת יְהוֹשָׁפָ֑ט וַיָּ֧נַֽח ל֦וֹ אֱלֹהָ֖יו מִסָּבִֽיב׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the kingdom of Josaphat was quiet, and God gave him peace round about.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin Josaphatin valtakunta oli levossa; ja Jumala antoi hänelle levon ympäristöllänsä.
verses.king_james: So the realm of Jehoshaphat was quiet: for his God gave him rest round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Regnavit igitur Josaphat super Judam, et erat triginta quinque annorum cum regnare cœpisset : viginti autem et quinque annis regnavit in Jerusalem, et nomen matris ejus Azuba filia Selahi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּמְלֹ֥ךְ יְהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט עַל־יְהוּדָ֑ה בֶּן־שְׁלֹשִׁים֩ וְחָמֵ֨שׁ שָׁנָ֜ה בְּמׇלְכ֗וֹ וְעֶשְׂרִ֨ים וְחָמֵ֤שׁ שָׁנָה֙ מָלַ֣ךְ בִּירוּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם וְשֵׁ֣ם אִמּ֔וֹ עֲזוּבָ֖ה בַּת־שִׁלְחִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Josaphat reigned over Juda, and he was five and thirty years old, when he began to reign: and he reigned five and twenty years in Jerusalem: and the name of his mother was Azuba the daughter of Selahi.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josaphat hallitsi Juudaa, ja oli viiden ajastaikainen neljättäkymmentä tullessansa kuninkaaksi ja hallitsi Jerusalemissa viisikolmattakymmentä ajastaikaa; hänen äitinsä nimi oli Asuba Silhin tytär.
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat reigned over Judah: he was thirty and five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty and five years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Azubah the daughter of Shilhi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ambulavit in via patris suis Asa, nec declinavit ab ea, faciens quæ placita erant coram Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֗לֶךְ בְּדֶ֛רֶךְ אָבִ֥יו אָסָ֖א וְלֹא־סָ֣ר מִמֶּ֑נָּה לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת הַיָּשָׁ֖ר בְּעֵינֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he walked in the way of his father Asa and departed not from it, doing the things that were pleasing before the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän vaelsi isänsä Asan teillä ja ei siitä horjahtanut, ja teki sitä mikä Herralle otollinen oli;
verses.king_james: And he walked in the way of Asa his father, and departed not from it, doing that which was right in the sight of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen excelsa non abstulit, et adhuc populus non direxerat cor suum ad Dominum Deum patrum suorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֥ךְ הַבָּמ֖וֹת לֹא־סָ֑רוּ וְע֤וֹד הָעָם֙ לֹא־הֵכִ֣ינוּ לְבָבָ֔ם לֵאלֹהֵ֖י אֲבֹתֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But yet he took not away the high places, and the people had not yet turned their heart to the Lord the God of their fathers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Paitsi ettei korkeudet tulleet otetuiksi pois; sillä ettei kansa vielä asettanut sydäntänsä Herran isänsä Jumalan tykö.
verses.king_james: Howbeit the high places were not taken away: for as yet the people had not prepared their hearts unto the God of their fathers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem gestorum Josaphat priorum et novissimorum scripta sunt in verbis Jehu filii Hanani, quæ digessit in libros regum Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֙תֶר֙ דִּבְרֵ֣י יְהוֹשָׁפָ֔ט הָרִאשֹׁנִ֖ים וְהָאַחֲרֹנִ֑ים הִנָּ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֗ים בְּדִבְרֵי֙ יֵה֣וּא בֶן־חֲנָ֔נִי אֲשֶׁ֣ר הֹֽעֲלָ֔ה עַל־סֵ֖פֶר מַלְכֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the rest of the acts of Josaphat, first and last, are written in the words of Jehu the son of Hanani, which he digested into the books of the kings of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä enempi Josaphatin töistä sanomista on, sekä ensimäisistä että viimeisistä: katso, ne ovat kirjoitetut Jehun Hananin pojan teoissa, jotka hän oli kirjoittanut Israelin kuningasten kirjaan.
verses.king_james: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of Jehu the son of Hanani, who is mentioned in the book of the kings of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc iniit amicitias Josaphat rex Juda cum Ochozia rege Israël, cujus opera fuerunt impiissima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַחֲרֵי־כֵ֗ן אֶתְחַבַּר֙ יְהוֹשָׁפָ֣ט מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֔ה עִ֖ם אֲחַזְיָ֣ה מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל ה֖וּא הִרְשִׁ֥יעַ לַעֲשֽׂוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: After these things Josaphat king of Juda made friendship with Ochozias king of Israel, whose works were very wicked.
verses.finnish_biblia: Senjälkeen yhdisti Josaphat Juudan kuningas itsensä Ahasian Israelin kuninkaan kanssa, joka oli jumalatoin menoissansa.
verses.king_james: And after this did Jehoshaphat king of Judah join himself with Ahaziah king of Israel, who did very wickedly:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et particeps fuit ut facerent naves quæ irent in Tharsis : feceruntque classem in Asiongaber.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְחַבְּרֵ֣הוּ עִמּ֔וֹ לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת אֳנִיּ֖וֹת לָלֶ֣כֶת תַּרְשִׁ֑ישׁ וַיַּעֲשׂ֥וּ אֳנִיּ֖וֹת בְּעֶצְי֥וֹן גָּֽבֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he was partner with him in making ships, to go to Tharsis: and they made the ships in Asiongaber.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän suostui hänen kanssansa haaksia tekemään, vaeltaaksensa Tarsikseen. Ja he tekivät haaksia EtseonGeberissä.
verses.king_james: And he joined himself with him to make ships to go to Tarshish: and they made the ships in Ezion–geber.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prophetavit autem Eliezer filius Dodau de Maresa ad Josaphat, dicens : Quia habuisti fœdus cum Ochozia, percussit Dominus opera tua, contritæque sunt naves, nec potuerunt ire in Tharsis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתְנַבֵּ֞א אֱלִיעֶ֤זֶר בֶּן־דֹּדָוָ֙הוּ֙ מִמָּ֣רֵשָׁ֔ה עַל־יְהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט לֵאמֹ֑ר כְּהִֽתְחַבֶּרְךָ֣ עִם־אֲחַזְיָ֗הוּ פָּרַ֤ץ יְהֹוָה֙ אֶֽת־מַעֲשֶׂ֔יךָ וַיִּשָּׁבְר֣וּ אֳנִיּ֔וֹת וְלֹ֥א עָצְר֖וּ לָלֶ֥כֶת אֶל־תַּרְשִֽׁישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Eliezer the son of Dodau of Maresa prophesied to Josaphat, saying: Because thou hast made a league with Ochozias, the Lord hath destroyed thy works, and the ships are broken, and they could not go to Tharsis.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Elieser Dodavan poika Maresasta ennusti Josaphatia vastaan ja sanoi: ettäs olet suostunut Ahasiaan, on Herra särkenyt sinun työs; ja haahdet menivät rikki, ja ei he saaneet enää Tarsikseen kulkea.
verses.king_james: Then Eliezer the son of Dodavah of Mareshah prophesied against Jehoshaphat, saying, Because thou hast joined thyself with Ahaziah, the Lord hath broken thy works. And the ships were broken, that they were not able to go to Tarshish.
